_meta.purpose: Canonical, machine-readable identity card for the Binaural Test Lab (BTL). Locks the single canonical identity of BTL across all repositories and downstream documents. Issued to correct dual-gloss errors that appeared in earlier README transcripts.

companion_document.name: RWA Corporate Reference v1.0
companion_document.relationship: The two RWA-001 documents are complementary: the corporate reference covers company name, location, contact, licensing, and personnel; this lab identity card covers scientific identity (BTL = Binaural Test Lab single canonical), research outputs (DADC/DADI/ADAC/H₁), and downstream lineage to HUF/Hˢ/CNT/CNQ. Same RWA-001 ID, two facets.

lab.abbreviation_meaning: Binaural Test Lab (single canonical identity, no other gloss)
lab.explicit_clarification: BTL is NOT 'Below Threshold Loudspeaker'. Earlier README transcripts that suggested a dual gloss were errors. The canonical identity, established by the lab itself, is Binaural Test Lab and only Binaural Test Lab.
lab.facility_class: Sound-controlled professional laboratory with advanced-systems test facilities engineered for real-world four-way active reference monitor development, closed-loop measurement-driven validation, and DADC/DADI/ADAC compositional analysis.
lab.purpose: Loudspeaker measurement, four-way active reference monitoring, closed-loop validation, and the empirical work that produced the simplex/compositional thinking later formalised as the Higgins Operator H1 and the broader Hs/CNT/CNQ framework.

lab_network._description: BTL is a laboratory class with multiple deployments. The research/originating lab is operated by Peter Higgins; the design has been deployed institutionally by a private Canadian industrial sponsor as a four-laboratory test network across two countries.

research_lab.status: Currently operational — RWA's primary research and certification facility
research_lab.status_note: Currently sited within a residence as a continuation of work during and after the COVID-19 period. The siting is incidental; the facility itself is fully equipped to professional standards (sound control, calibrated measurement chain, real test instrumentation) regardless of physical location.
research_lab.history: Originally developed at the manufacturing facility of the private Canadian industrial sponsor that funded the project (pre-COVID). Relocation during the pandemic was a forced decision that has remained operationally effective and was retained.

institutional_deployments._description: The private Canadian industrial sponsor maintains a four-laboratory deployment of the BTL design for advanced-systems test work.
institutional_deployments.sponsor_description: Private Canadian industrialist who funded the original DADC/DADI/ADAC and BTL design programme; identity withheld here pending the sponsor's preferred public attribution.
institutional_deployments.scope: International institutional deployment (Canada + Monaco), continuously operational.

lab_network.framing_note: When BTL is referenced in academic, regulatory, or technical writing, it should be described as a sound-controlled professional laboratory class with both research (Markham) and institutional (Ottawa, Monaco) deployments. Earlier informal descriptions of the Markham facility as a 'basement lab' captured a true incidental property of its current siting but materially understate the lab's professional character and the broader BTL network. Use 'professional laboratory' or 'sound-controlled laboratory' in formal writing.

contact_authority._role: RWA-001 is the canonical CONTACT AUTHORITY for the system (RWA, HUF, Hs). Any JSON or document that needs contact/identity data references THIS card; it does not carry its own copy. Single source of truth for contact/identity, per the document-distribution rule (DOCUMENT_DISTRIBUTION.md).
contact_authority.gate: Peter Higgins -- sole commit and contact gate; no AI contacts or commits on its own.
contact_authority.personal_contact: WITHHELD -- carrier-filter: personal addresses are not published in the repositories. Route through the business address or Peter directly.
contact_authority.corporate_companion: RWA Corporate Reference v1.0 (corporate / legal / licensing / personnel facet of RWA-001) -- see _meta.companion_document.
contact_authority.how_to_use: If a file needs contact data, reference 'RWA-001 (RWA repo RWA-001.json) contact_authority' instead of embedding an address. On any conflict about contact/identity, THIS card wins.

DADC.name: Dimension-Apportioned Diffraction Correction
DADC.summary: Apportionment of a fixed 6.02 dB cabinet-edge diffraction budget across three cabinet dimensions (width, height, depth). The first natural simplex constraint in the Higgins lineage.

DADI.name: Dimension-Apportioned Diffraction Inference
DADI.summary: Inverse mapping — given measured response, recover the dimensional apportionment.

ADAC.name: Adaptive Closure
ADAC.summary: Adaptive closure rule that maintains the simplex constraint under varying conditions. Third member of the DADC family.

Higgins_Operator_H1.name: Higgins Operator H₁
Higgins_Operator_H1.summary: Nonlinear unity-normalization map on Hilbert space that enforces strict global unity normalization (∑ = 1) across hierarchical regimes while preserving directional coherence. The first formal mathematical generalization of DADC.

BTL_Build_Guide_v1.0.name: Binaural Test Lab — Build Guide v1.0
BTL_Build_Guide_v1.0.summary: Comprehensive production-quality guide for constructing and calibrating the BTL four-way active reference monitoring system.

downstream_lineage.lineage_chain: DADC → H₁ (Higgins Operator) → HUF (MC-4 + EITT) → Hˢ (Higgins Decomposition) → CNT (Compositional Navigation Tensor) → CNQ (Compositional Navigation Quaternion) → HCI-AUDIO + HCI-ULTRASOUND (applied tiers)

system_specifications.diffraction_apportionment: across three cabinet dimensions (width, height, depth) — DADC simplex constraint
system_specifications.drivers: 32W woofer, M15CH002 midrange, Exotic T35 upper-mid, R2904 tweeter (per Build Guide v1.0)
system_specifications.design_philosophy: Organic Digital Loudspeakers v2.6 — selecting naturally compatible drivers and applying minimal mathematical correction to maintain transparency and neutrality

errata_record.2026-05-08: RWA-001 issued. Corrects dual-gloss error in two RWA-mirror documents (README.md line 17, concepts/btl-lab-study/BTL_Advanced_ODL_Test_Platform.md line 11) where 'Below Threshold Loudspeaker' had appeared as an apparent expansion. Both files updated. Same correction applied in the Hs repo to ORIGIN_DADC_LINEAGE.md, AI_REFRESH_2026-05-08_grok_crosscheck.md, AI_REFRESH_2026-05-08_push24_grok_crosscheck.md, and HS_ADMIN.json.
